title: Hiroshima Peace Memorial
narrative: The iconic Genbaku Dome, preserved as a monument to the devastation of nuclear weapons. While distinct from RAF Bomber Command memorials, it represents the broader context of strategic bombing in WW2.

external_narratives.summary: The Hiroshima Peace Memorial (Genbaku Dome) was inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage List in December 1996. It is the only structure left standing near the hypocentre of the atomic bomb detonated on 6 August 1945, preserved in its post-explosion condition as a symbol of peace and nuclear disarmament.
key_details: Only structure surviving near bomb hypocentre

external_narratives.summary: The Hiroshima Peace Memorial Museum provides the official interpretive context for the Genbaku Dome and the broader peace memorial park. Three conservation projects (1967, 1989-1990, 2002-2003) have used minimal reinforcement to preserve the dome's condition.

sources: Photon.komoot.io — Hiroshima Peace Memorial Park OSM relation at 34.3932 132.4523
location_confidence.verification_notes: MINOR CORRECTION. Current coords (34.39, 132.45) are rounded approximation ~300m from Peace Memorial Park centroid. Updated to OSM park centroid 34.3932, 132.4523.
